data.title: Palantir Stock (PLTR) Just Had Its Best Day in a Year. Here's Why
data.description:
The stock of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) closed 9% higher on May 29, its best one-day performance in a year.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The rally was sparked by a rebound in software stocks. Specifically, Palantir's stock got a boost from rally in the stock of Snowflake (SNOW) following that company's blowout earnings report earlier in the week. Snowflake's earnings sparked a sector-wide rally that has lasted for two consecutive trading sessions.

Palantir's stock has gained 13% over the last week, its best performance since April 2025, according to market data. Palantir, Snowflake and other software companies have seen their share prices take a beating this year over worries that artificial intelligence (AI) will disrupt their business models.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: S&P 500 Top Stock Gainers Today, 5/29/26
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) advanced to a new record high on Friday, closing out May with a 4.8% gain.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Let's break down the sectors behind the performance.

Today's Top-Performing Sector 

Information technology was the top-performing sector on Friday. Dell Technologies (DELL) led the charge after reporting a blowout first-quarter earnings report, highlighted by AI server revenue growth of 757% to $16.1 billion. In addition, software stocks continued to rally higher, with NetApp (NTAP), IBM (IBM), and ServiceNow (NOW) posting strong returns.

Several information technology stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Dell Technologies (DELL)
  • NetApp (NTAP)
  • IBM (IBM)
  • ServiceNow (NOW)
  • Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the consumer staples sector faced the largest drawdown as investors rotated out of the defensive sector and into more speculative areas of the market. Clorox (CLX) led the group to the downside after CEO Linda Rendle announced she would step down due to health reasons and start a search for the next CEO. Costco (COST) also closed lower after reporting its fiscal third-quarter earnings.

Notable consumer staples stocks trading lower include:

  • Clorox (CLX)
  • Costco (COST)
  • Altria Group (MO)
  • Kimberly-Clark Corp (KMB)
  • Walmart (WMT)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Bitcoin's (BTC) Monthly Win Streak Ends as It Falls 4% in May
data.description:
Bitcoin's (BTC) monthly win streak has come to an end.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

BTC ended May down 4% on the month. The largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization had risen in both March and April of this year before sliding lower during May. In late day trading on May 29, Bitcoin's price was at $73,000, down from a peak of $82,000 earlier in the month.

Analysts say that cryptocurrencies continue to be hurt by geopolitical uncertainty, notably the Iran war. There is also weak demand for digital assets among institutional investors. Over the past nine trading sessions, investors have pulled $2.8 billion from about a dozen Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs), the most since the investment vehicles were launched two years ago.

Ethereum Falls Too

Bitcoin isn't alone in its May decline. Ethereum (ETH), the second largest digital asset, fell 12% during the month as investors headed to the sidelines. Ethereum's price has fallen 5% in the past five trading sessions and is down 33% on the year.

Most worrisome, Ethereum's price recently fell below the key support level of $2,000 that analysts say is critically important. Skeptics say that there is likely to be further downside in Ethereum now that the price has dropped below $2,000.

BTC's Three-Month Performance

Despite the selloff in recent days, Bitcoin has recovered from its year-to-date lows and risen 13% in the past 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Surges as GM Throws Down an Investor Gauntlet
data.description:
Legacy automaker Ford (F) has a dilemma on its hands, and it is one that looks good for shareholders. New reports revealed that General Motors (GM) has been handing out cash like candy to its shareholders, and Ford shareholders are looking to see if it will follow suit. Ford shares blasted up nearly 6% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

GM took a bold approach to keeping shareholder interest. Not only did it launch a $6 billion buyback back in January, it also hiked its dividend. Ford, meanwhile, engaged in some buybacks of its own, about $311 million worth. That was only a fraction of what GM rolled out. Ford also left its dividend alone. There was a clear difference here in philosophy, and some are wondering just what Ford is waiting for.

Ford may have answered that question recently with Ford Energy, the new venture that drove a lot of Ford's gains in the last week. Ford is also pouring cash into Ford Pro and the Model e program, which is still showing substantial losses. Some might think that is a prudent response to a changing market. Others might think it is unnecessarily strict. Which of these is the actual answer, meanwhile, remains to be seen.

Ford Hits Union Station

It should surprise no one that Ford is poised to celebrate America250, and is doing something just a little out of the ordinary. Union Station is a big deal, with some regarding it as "…the front door to the nation's capital." But this platform that mostly focuses on trains will instead be home to a set of vehicles and artifacts as part of a free public exhibit called Driving America Forward: A Ford Experience at Union Station.

The event will be split into seven sections, starting with the birth of Ford and how Ford got the world into driving. Another will deal with Ford on the job, how Ford vehicles help make so much of the world run. Pop culture, war, racing, and even unexpected innovations will all be part of the exhibit, simultaneously celebrating Ford and the United States at the same time.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 10 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 60.4% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.85 per share implies 20.79% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Universal Music (UMG) Rejects Bill Ackman's $65 Billion Takeover Offer
data.description:
Universal Music Group (UMG) has rejected an unsolicited $65 billion takeover offer from Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital Management (PS).

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The board of directors at Universal Music said that the offer significantly undervalued the company and failed to serve the best interests of its shareholders and other stakeholders. Universal Music said its board unanimously decided to reject the takeover offer submitted by Pershing Square on April 7 of this year.

The board added that said there was strong support from shareholders and for the decision company to reject Ackman's offer to buy Universal Music. For Bill Ackman, a prominent hedge fund manager with a big social media following, the rejection is a blow.

The Next Warren Buffett?

Ackman's bid for Universal Music comes as the investor says he is trying to turn his company Pershing Square into a diversified holding company and investment vehicle similar to Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B). Ackman has praised Berkshire Hathaway's Warren Buffett and his investing acumen and track record.

In April, Bill Ackman had proposed a cash-and-stock offer that valued Universal Music at €30.40 per share, making the ​deal worth €55.75 billion (US$65.03 billion). In a news release, Universal Board Board Chair Sherry Lansing said the company remains confident in its long-term strategy.

Is UMG Stock a Buy?

Universal Music Group's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 10 analysts. That rating is based on seven Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average UMG price target of €26.01 implies 33% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: UnitedHealth (UNH) Hit with State Lawsuit over Alleged $100M MassHealth Overbilling
data.description:
UnitedHealth's (UNH) insurance unit has been sued by Massachusetts for allegedly defrauding MassHealth, the state's Medicaid program, out of more than $100 million. The complaint alleged that between 2015 and 2025, UnitedHealthcare engaged in systematic "upcoding" to support what the state calls a "growth‑at‑all‑costs" strategy

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

According to the Attorney General's office, the insurer boosted profits through several improper practices:

  • Falsifying Illnesses: The company allegedly exaggerated the health conditions of older patients in its Senior Care Options (SCO) plan to make them appear sicker than they were.
  • Fabricating Diagnoses: Labeling healthy members as "Level 2" by claiming they had behavioral health issues like anxiety or depression without any real diagnosis or treatment.
  • Misclassifying patients: The insurer placed members into "Level 3," the tier for the sickest patients needing daily skilled nursing care, even though they did not require those services.
  • Keeping known overpayments: Internal reviews in 2018 and 2019 flagged these errors, but instead of returning the excess funds, the company allegedly downgraded member statuses while keeping the money.

UnitedHealthcare is the largest SCO provider in Massachusetts, serving low‑income residents aged 65 and older. The lawsuit accuses the company of submitting false claims and unjust enrichment. Attorney General Andrea Joy Campbell is seeking to recover the overpayments and pursue triple damages under state law.

The lawsuit comes as UnitedHealth is facing a challenging regulatory period in its history. The company has faced strict scrutiny since the Change Healthcare cyberattack, multiple probes into its Medicare Advantage billing practices, and growing attention on how large insurers manage government‑funded health plans.

Is UNH Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on UNH stock based on 18 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months. Further, the average UnitedHealth price target of $397.05 per share implies 4.16% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slides Despite New G-Series Potential
data.description:
Yesteday, we noted that chip stock Intel (INTC) was bringing its Arc G-Series processor to new gaming handhelds from Acer, the Predator Atlas 8. This revelation prompted some to wonder if they G-series lineup could be a big new source of revenue for Intel in a time when all eyes are on its bottom line. Investors seemed oddly concerned, and pulled back, sending Intel shares down nearly 2.5% in Friday afternoon's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The new chips bring together potent graphics capabilities alongside highly efficient power management systems. That combination makes them tailor-made for handheld gaming systems, where graphics demand balances on a razor's edge against battery demand. Intel's G-Series has a string of other tools that help out in this regard, including an available Xbox mode to offer a console-style experience, and Intel Precompiled Shaders to make games load faster.

Naturally, Intel does not have this market to itself. Both Qualcomm (QCOM) and AMD (AMD) have chips in this space. Qualcomm's Snapdragon Elite Gaming system is proving to be a significant presence, and AMD has perennially been an Intel competitor. The Ryzen Z2 processor is proving to be a powerhouse in portable gaming, so Intel will not have things all its own way here.

Keeping Things Cool

Perhaps sensing the upcoming summer heat in North America, and knowing that people will spend a large portion of it indoors, Intel recently offered up a set of new Linux patches geared toward Directed Package Thermal Interrupts. Intel first sent out patches addressing these interrupts back in March, and a second version landed just recently.

Directed Package Thermal Interrupts allow package thermal interrupts to be sent to one core alone. This in turn allows for fewer cores getting involved, less demand for resources, and a reduced need to accidentally fire up idle cores when only one core could do the job. So far, reports suggest, only newer Intel processors will have access to Directed Package Thermal Interrupts, and several upcoming processors—Nova Lake and Diamond Rapids—will have it on board as well.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on 10 Buys, 25 Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 518.36% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $86.94 per share implies 26.41% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: SpaceX Secures $4.16 Billion Space Force Contract Ahead of IPO
data.description:
The U.S. Space Force has awarded Elon Musk's SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract for space-based satellites ahead of the company's upcoming initial public offering (IPO).

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Specifically, the contract is for the Space-Based Airborne Moving Target Indicator program, which is designed to track airborne threats to the U.S. from space. Going forward, SpaceX will provide global tracking capabilities to U.S. military forces.

Government officials in Washington, D.C. say the program addresses challenges faced by the U.S. military as adversaries develop increasingly sophisticated missiles and airborne attack systems, many of which can bypass traditional detection systems.

"By focusing these capabilities to the space domain, we are providing the Joint Force with sustained battlespace awareness of contested airspace," said Col. Ryan Frazier, acting Space Force portfolio acquisition executive for SBST.

The SB-AMTI system integrates space-based sensors, communication links, and ground processing. The Space Force is using a hybrid acquisition model combining an OTA agreement with an Indefinite Delivery/Indefinite Quantity structure.

The Space Force has established a multi-vendor framework for the program. SpaceX and other companies were selected for the vendor pool through competitive OTAs announced by Secretary of the Air Force Troy Meink at Space Symposium in April.

The initial award is projected to deploy a satellite constellation by 2028. The Space Force plans to issue additional awards in the coming year to expand the program's capacity.

Space Systems Command manages a $15.6 billion annual space acquisition budget for the Department of War.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 777X in 2027? FAA Suggests So, as Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Rises
data.description:
More good news hit for aerospace stock Boeing (BA) as the Federal Aviation Administration seems ready to start clearing new Boeing aircraft. The outlook was optimistic, and investors followed along, sending Boeing shares up modestly in Friday afternoon's trading.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

You might remember yesterday, when Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg noted that the 737-7 was likely to be certified ahead of the 737-10. That sentiment was echoed, at least somewhat, by FAA administrator Bryan Bedford. Bedford noted that Ortberg's projections were right, saying that the MAX 7 was likely to come first, followed by the MAX 10. But Bedford took things one step farther, saying, "…hopefully the 777 early next year."

This came as a bit of a surprise, as Boeing was looking for an amended type certificate before the end of 2026. That would have opened up the potential for deliveries to start in early 2027. But if the certification does not arrive until 2027, then that likely means the delays will continue. The flight test department's workload has been "heavy," reports note, and with two other planes likely to be certified ahead of the 777X, that may explain why.

Repurposed Hardpoints

Meanwhile, reports note that Boeing is working to get more life out of the B-1B lineup by improving weapons capacity. Using a new feature call the Load Adaptable Modular multi-use weapons pylon, Boeing could effectively turn bombers in missile-carriers as well, especially long-range hypersonic missiles, reports note.

The pylon has not yet been certified, but Boeing is working on getting through the design phase that will see formerly-used hardpoints—points where weapons can be mounted—replaced with the new pylon. The pylon has been tested as far back as 2022, and tests continued into 2024, finding that the B-1B could indeed carry long-range munitions.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 15 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.35% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $274.14 per share implies 18.64% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
